summary: NEW FEATURES VERIFIED: (1) PWA (manifest.json + sw.js) and (2) Web Push Notifications end-to-end. Backend: 47/47 pytest tests pass sequentially (added 10 new tests: 8 TestPush + 2 TestPWA, plus 37 regression). All new push endpoints work: GET /api/push/vapid-public-key returns a valid VAPID public key; POST /api/push/subscribe upserts a subscription; GET /api/push/status transitions False->True->False correctly; POST /api/push/unsubscribe removes the sub; POST /api/push/check-reminders returns {sent:int}; POST /api/push/test returns 400 with Italian detail when no valid subscription can deliver. All push endpoints correctly require auth (401 when anonymous). Subscribe rejects payload without endpoint (400). GET /manifest.json returns 200 valid JSON (name=PawCare, display=standalone, start_url=/dashboard, icons 192+512). GET /sw.js returns 200 JS with push+showNotification handlers. Frontend (E2E via public URL as admin@pawcare.it): logged in via submit-auth-button, service worker registered at /sw.js (regs.count=1), manifest link tag present in <head>. Header shows notifications-toggle button (visible, enabled) after login. On dashboard load, /api/pets + /api/dashboard/upcoming + /api/push/check-reminders all fire correctly (checkReminders wired to Dashboard.jsx). Clicking notifications-toggle does not error in the headless browser; as expected the Notification.requestPermission() cannot be granted in a non-user-gesture headless env, so subsequent POST /api/push/subscribe is not observed — this is a browser limitation, NOT a bug (per review request). Regression: pet CRUD via UI works (created + deleted TEST_UITemp via add-pet-button -> save-pet-button -> delete-pet-button flow, toast 'Animale eliminato' shown); pet detail exposes tab-visits, tab-vaccines, tab-treatments, tab-weight; Guides page loads Italian content; Assistente AI page shows chat-input + chat-send-button. AI chat memory verified in backend (3-turn recall of 'Golden Retriever' and 'orecchie' passes). No console errors observed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /push/test: returns generic 400 'Nessuna notifica inviata' regardless of whether the user had no subscriptions or all pushes failed. Consider distinguishing (a) no subscriptions vs (b) all subscriptions were pruned as dead — the frontend could then prompt the user to (a) enable notifications vs (b) re-subscribe.

server.py send_push_to_user: dead-subscription pruning deletes by user_id+endpoint, but a re-subscription in the SAME request cycle isn't possible; if a user has multiple stale subs, they will each be pruned one-by-one on next check — fine, but consider a bulk delete_many on WebPushException 410/404.

server.py push_check_reminders: the dedupe key uses `today_iso` (send-date), so the same reminder is re-sent every calendar day until the due date passes. That's reasonable, but consider suppressing repeats within N hours if user has multiple devices producing spam.
server.py CORS: allow_origins is [FRONTEND_URL] but backend/.env has both CORS_ORIGINS='*' (unused) and FRONTEND_URL — remove CORS_ORIGINS to avoid config drift.
server.py FastAPI 'on_event' startup/shutdown is deprecated — migrate to lifespan context manager.
server.py `requests` is a sync library used inside async /auth/google/session — will block the event loop under load; use httpx.AsyncClient.
backend/.env: VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY is stored in plaintext; ensure it's not committed. (Fine for preview, flag for production hardening.)

pytest.ini still forces `-n 2 --dist loadscope` — this remains flaky for module-scoped shared fixtures across TestPets/TestRecords. Serial `-n 0` remains the recommended dev run and was used here (47/47 green).

Class-scoped fixtures on instance methods still trigger PytestRemovedIn10Warning (TestAIChatMemory.fresh_chat_session, TestWeights.weight_user, TestPush.push_user). Convert to @classmethod or module-level fixture.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added TestPush with 8 tests + TestPWA with 2 tests; final total 47)

success_rate.frontend: 100% of PWA + push UI wiring + regression

seed_data_creation: TestPush uses a fresh ephemeral user 'test_push_*@example.com' with synthetic FCM subscription payloads (no real delivery). Frontend UI regression created and cascade-deleted an ephemeral pet 'TEST_UITemp'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run backend suite serially: `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/backend_test.py -n 0 -v` (47/47 in ~57s). Push endpoints: /api/push/{vapid-public-key,subscribe,unsubscribe,status,test,check-reminders}. Synthetic subs use endpoint='https://fcm.googleapis.com/fcm/send/fake-*' with keys.p256dh (base64url, ~88 chars) + keys.auth (~22 chars). Real push delivery is NOT automatable in headless — verify only endpoint contracts + UI button presence + client-side wiring. Frontend notifications-toggle button lives in Layout.jsx and only renders when pushSupported()==true (needs serviceWorker+PushManager+Notification in navigator — chromium headless supports all three). SW registers via /sw.js in src/index.js; manifest linked from public/index.html. Dashboard.jsx auto-invokes checkReminders() on mount (fire-and-forget POST /api/push/check-reminders, errors swallowed).
rca of the issue: N/A — both PWA + push features work as specified end-to-end. Clicking the notifications-toggle in headless does not complete a real subscription because Notification.requestPermission() cannot be granted without a user gesture / persistent origin permission, but this is a browser environment limitation explicitly excluded from the failure criteria in the review request.
